calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Return Of Cash Deposit
Off Calendar Apr-09-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the Court adopts the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT YELLOW CAB COOPERATIVE, INC.'s Motion For Return Of Cash Deposit is granted. No opposition filed and good cause shown.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Christine Jordan, CSR#12262, email: cjordan@sftc.org.

ORDER SIGNED AFTER HEARING.

ORDER OF EXAMINATION ISSUED
Off Calendar Oct-20-2015 Discharged

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment
Off Calendar Oct-15-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. MICHAEL RAIFSNIDER, ESQ. of Raifsnider Law Offices, Tel No. 415-398-9001 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. TIMOTHY C. WILSON, ESQ. of Aaron & Wilson, LLP, Tel No. 415-438-7802 appearing for Defendant Yellow Cab Cooperative, Inc. (Wrongfully sued as "Yellow Cab").

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Yellow Cab's Motion to Set Aside Default Judgment is DENIED. It is the "policy of the law to favor, wherever possible, a hearing on the merits . . . ." (Weitz v. Yankosky (1966) 63 Cal.2d 849, 854-55.) "When service of a summons has not resulted in actual notice to a party . . . she may serve and file a notice of motion to set aside the default . . . The notice of motion shall be served and filed within a reasonable time, but in no event exceeding the earlier of: (i) two years after entry of a default judgment against him or her; or (ii) 180 days after service on him or her of a written notice that the default or default judgment has been entered." (Code Civ. Proc., 473.5.) Two years after the Court entered default judgment is September 10, 2015. One hundred eighty days after Plaintiff served Defendant Yellow Cab with written notice of the judgment is June 2, 2014. The earlier of these days is June 2, 2014. Defendant Yellow Cab filed its instant motion on September 9, 2015. That is 644 days after Plaintiff served Defendant Yellow Cab with written notice of the judgment, and too late under section 473.5. [C]ourts must presume that a legislature says in a statute what it means and means in a statute what it says there." (Connecticut Nat'l Bank v. Germain (1992) 503 U.S. 249, 253-254.) "When the words of a statute are unambiguous, then, this first canon is also the last: 'judicial inquiry is complete.' " (Id., at p. 254, citing Rubin v. United States (1981) 449 U.S. 424, 430.). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)
